title: AGENTS.md
text: pkgskills — AI Skills R Package Development collection curated, opinionated skills agent instructions improve agentic coding R packages. https://github.com/api2r/pkgskills, https://api2r.github.io/pkgskills/

title: AGENTS.md
text: project follows standard R package conventions key directories: pkgskills/ ├── R/ # R source code │ ├── pkgskills-package.R # Auto-generated package docs │ ├── aaa-shared_params.R # Shared @inheritParams definitions │ ├── aaa-conditions.R # Auto-generated package docs │ ├── utils.R # Shared internal helpers │ └── *.R # Function definitions, 1 file ~= 1 exported function ├── inst/templates/ # Templates agent setup ├── .github/ │ ├── ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ # GitHub issue templates │ ├── skills/ # Agent skill definitions │ └── workflows/ # CI/CD configurations ├── tests/testthat/ # Test suite ├── man/ # Generated documentation ├── AGENTS.md # Main agent setup file ├── DESCRIPTION # Package metadata ├── NAMESPACE # Auto-generated export information ├── NEWS.md # Changelog └── Various config files # .gitignore, codecov.yml, etc.

title: AGENTS.md
text: feature, fix, refactor: Update packages: pak::pak() Run tests — confirm passing changes: devtools::test(reporter = "check"). fail, stop ask. Plan — identify affected R files; check new exports needed (→ r-code skill). Test first — write failing test, implement (→ tdd-workflow skill): devtools::test(filter = "name", reporter = "check"). Implement — minimal code pass tests. Refactor — clean , keep tests green. Document — use document skill new/changed exports. Verify: Run air format ., wrapping : devtools::check(error_on = "warning"). Resolve warnings, errors, NOTEs. News — add bullet top NEWS.md (dev heading): User-facing changes . 1 line, end . Present tense, positive framing, function names (backticks + ()) near start: * `fn()` now accepts ... * Fixed ... Issue/contributor final period: * `fn()` now accepts ... (@user, #N). #N GitHub issue number implemented (e.g. #42). Get username: gh api user --jq .login; get issue number user’s prompt, branch name (git branch --show-current), gh issue list. Never guess invent issue number. writing , verify: (1) received user branch name, (2) looked gh. trace number concrete source, use #noissue.

title: AGENTS.md

title: AGENTS.md
text: R console: use --quiet --vanilla. Always run air format . generating R code. Comments explain , . writing reviewing code, load relevant skills (usually r-code, tdd-workflow, document).

title: Contributing to pkgskills
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("api2r/pkgskills", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to pkgskills
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Using agentic AI for R package development
text: AI coding agents can remarkably effective contributors R package development, right context. Without project-specific guidance, even powerful models tend produce code syntactically correct stylistically inconsistent, ignoring package’s conventions, testing patterns, design philosophy. pkgskills addresses drawback installing structured markdown files project: AGENTS.md set skills. files encode opinionated decisions make R packages consistent maintainable. walking use package, help clarify four terms come throughout article: Model: underlying AI (e.g., Claude Sonnet 4.6). large language model (LLM) general-purpose; broad knowledge awareness specific project, conventions, toolchain. Agent: model plus scaffold gives tools (file access, shell execution, web search, etc.) context. Different agents can powered model behave differently depending context tools given. example, writing Posit Assistant (RStudio) GitHub Copilot Coding Agent can use Claude Sonnet 4.6 underlying model, yet behave quite differently: Posit Assistant interactive conversational, Copilot Coding Agent works autonomously background GitHub issue. agents used develop pkgskills. Tool: capability agent scaffold exposes model, reading writing files, executing shell commands, searching web. Tools distinguish agent simple chat interface; allow model take actions project rather just generating text. Skill: structured markdown file gives agent project-specific, opinionated guidance particular task. Skills bridge gap general-purpose model specific conventions project. framing explains pkgskills exists: underlying model can produce different results depending context given. pkgskills delivery mechanism context R package development.

what: use_ai(): one call to set everything up
title: Using agentic AI for R package development
text: quickest way get started single call use_ai(): call installs three things: AGENTS.md (via use_agent()): top-level context file every agent reads. describes repository layout, standard workflow, find skills. GitHub Copilot workflow file (via use_github_copilot()): copilot-setup-steps.yml workflow companion reusable install action, Copilot Coding Agent can work repository. Seven skills installed .github/skills/ default: create-issue, document, github, implement-issue, r-code, search-code, tdd-workflow. full signature : Key parameters: skills: subset skills get installed. overwrite: whether overwrite existing files (default FALSE). open: whether open AGENTS.md editing installation.
code: pkgskills::use_ai() pkgskills::use_ai(   save_agent_as = "AGENTS.md",   target_skills_dir = ".github",   use_skills_subdir = TRUE,   overwrite = FALSE,   open = rlang::is_interactive(),   gh_token = gh::gh_token(),   skills = c(     "create-issue", "document", "github", "implement-issue",     "r-code", "search-code", "tdd-workflow"   ) )

title: Using agentic AI for R package development
text: Skills markdown files agent loads demand trigger phrase appears conversation. skill provides opinionated, project-specific guidance beyond general-purpose agent already knows. installed skill registered ## Skills table AGENTS.md every agent knows skills exist load , even agent doesn’t normally look location skills saved. opinions encoded skills arbitrary. reflect conventions described R Packages (2e) package structure workflow, Tidyverse style guide code formatting naming, Tidy design principles (opinions patterns writers package) API design. embedding references skills, pkgskills helps ensure agents produce functional code consistent broader R package ecosystem.

title: Using agentic AI for R package development
text: pkgskills ships seven skills: create-issue: creates well-structured GitHub issues via gh CLI GraphQL API; enforces conventional-commit titles standardized body format. implement-issue: end--end workflow implementing GitHub issue: fetch issue, plan, write tests first, implement, document, run checks, open pull request. r-code: opinionated guidance R function design, error handling, code patterns package development. Draws Tidy design principles API design decisions Tidyverse style guide code style. tdd-workflow: test-driven development: write failing test, implement pass, refactor. document: opinionated roxygen2 documentation standards, including shared-parameter setup via @inheritParams. search-code: AST-based code search refactoring using astgrepr. github: gh CLI usage conventional commit message conventions.

what: A workflow for agentic R package development
title: Using agentic AI for R package development
text: skills designed support specific development workflow. practice, developing R package AI agents looks like : Write clear issue. Use create-issue skill (installed via use_skill_create_issue()) produce well-structured GitHub issue. quality issue determines quality agent’s output. vague issue (“add function X”) forces agent guess; well-structured issue proposed function signature, argument descriptions, expected behavior, edge cases gives agent everything needs produce high-quality code minimal back--forth. development package, spent majority development time iterating Posit Assistant ensure issues clear. Let agent implement . Point agent issue (either assigning Copilot Coding Agent asking local agent like Posit Assistant Claude Code implement ). implement-issue skill guides full cycle: fetch issue, plan, write tests first, implement, document, run checks, open pull request issue. Review result. time spent writing clear issues refining skills, less time needs spent pull request reviews. may also find early issues require review time later issues, codebase can serve guide future development.

title: Using agentic AI for R package development
text: development pkgskills used Claude Sonnet 4.6 underlying model. GitHub Copilot Coding Agent used autonomous PR-based work, Posit Assistant RStudio (mostly Claude Sonnet 4.6, sometimes Claude Opus 4.6) used interactive, conversational development. following pull requests implemented GitHub Copilot Coding Agent (varying levels human involvement) real-world evidence well-structured skills issues enable: #8 #12 #13 #14 #16 #21 #22 #23 #24 #27 #29 contributions validate workflow produces meaningful, mergeable code agents given right context.

Determines template path install subdirectory. target_dir (character(1)) Directory skill installed, relative project root. Defaults ".github". trigger (character(1)) Trigger phrase skill. use_skills_subdir (logical(1)) Whether place skill folder skills subdirectory target_dir. Defaults TRUE, producing .github/skills/{skill}/SKILL.md. x_arg (character(1)) Argument name x, used error messages.

title: Set up the full AI agent suite for a project — use_ai
code: use_ai(   save_agent_as = "AGENTS.md",   target_skills_dir = ".github",   use_skills_subdir = TRUE,   overwrite = FALSE,   open = rlang::is_interactive(),   gh_token = gh::gh_token(),   skills = c("create-issue", "document", "github", "implement-issue", "r-code",     "search-code", "tdd-workflow") )

title: Set up the full AI agent suite for a project — use_ai
text: save_agent_as (character(1)) Output path AGENTS.md, relative project root. Passed use_agent() save_as. target_skills_dir (character(1)) Directory skills installed. Passed use_skill_*() functions target_dir. use_skills_subdir (logical(1)) Whether place skill folder skills subdirectory target_dir. Defaults TRUE, producing .github/skills/{skill}/SKILL.md. overwrite (logical(1)) Whether overwrite existing file. Defaults FALSE. open (logical(1)) Whether open file creation. gh_token (character(1)) GitHub personal access token. Defaults gh::gh_token(). skills (character) skills install. Defaults known skills. Pass subset install specific skills, character(0) none.

title: Set up the full AI agent suite for a project — use_ai
text: named list paths returned wrapped function, invisibly. Names match function name produced path (e.g. list(use_agent = ..., use_github_copilot = ..., use_skill_create_issue = ..., ...)). Skills selected omitted.

pkgskills (development version)
text: use_github_copilot() now installs container-based copilot-setup-steps.yml faster agent startup (#67). AGENTS.md tdd-workflow skill instructions now explicitly explain determine GitHub issue number warn agents never guess invent one (@copilot, #61). use_agent() template reconciled package’s AGENTS.md file (#59). tdd-workflow skill now documents stbl::expect_pkg_error_snapshot() explicit package argument instead helper-defined version (#51). Tests now use stbl::expect_pkg_error_snapshot() directly instead locally-defined wrapper; expect_pkg_error_snapshot() definition removed tests/testthat/helper-expectations.R (#50). use_skill_tdd_workflow() longer installs helper-expectations.R target project; stbl::expect_pkg_error_snapshot() now used directly (#52). use_skill_r_code() now installs minimal R/aaa-conditions.R project file already exist (#48). Internal coercion wrappers .to_string() .to_boolean() replaced direct stbl::to_character_scalar() stbl::to_lgl_scalar() calls; R/utils-coerce.R removed (#46). use_github_copilot() now writes workflow files byte--byte templates, preserving ${{ }} GitHub Actions expressions previously corrupted whisker rendering (#44). use_ai() now works called via pkgskills::use_ai() without first calling library(pkgskills) (#42). use_agent() now accepts overwrite argument (default FALSE) errors AGENTS.md already exists, consistent use_*() functions (#36). use_*() functions accept overwrite now default FALSE (#36). vignette("pkgskills") now provides complete “Get Started” guide covering terminology, use_ai() setup, skill descriptions, issue-writing best practices (#31). use_ai() sets full AI agent suite single call, installing AGENTS.md, GitHub Copilot workflow, selected skills (#28). use_github_copilot() installs copilot-setup-steps.yml workflow companion reusable install action .github/workflows/ (#25). use_skill_implement_issue() installs implement-issue skill target project (#18). use_skill_github() installs github skill, providing AI agents gh CLI guidance conventional commit message conventions (#17). use_skill_r_code() installs r-code skill target project (#17). use_skill_search_code() installs search-code skill target project (#20). use_skill_tdd_workflow() installs tdd-workflow skill bootstraps tests/testthat/helper-expectations.R target project (#11). use_skill_document() installs document skill , absent, creates starter R/aaa-shared_params.R file built-template (#9). use_skill_create_issue() installs create-issue skill, fetching repository metadata GitHub rendering tailored skill template project (#3). use_agent() installs structured AGENTS.md file, populating repository overview project’s DESCRIPTION (#2).

title: pkgskills 0.0.0.8000
text: Temporary bullet R CMD check ok NEWS.md first release.
